parent_entity.value: Restaurant Brands International Limited Partnership (RBILP), general partner Restaurant Brands International Inc. (RBI), Canadian corporation on NYSE/TSX

value: Corporate Addendum (Exhibit J1, for parent entities with Managing Director)
Fuel Co-Branded (Full Size, Large, Small, Kiosk)

tra.value: Target Reservation Agreement — $5,000 deposit, non-exclusive Target Area for single site search

mtra.value: Multiple Target Reservation Agreement — $10,000 deposit per committed opening, non-exclusive

development_agreement.value: Multi-unit commitment with development schedule. Prepay $50,000/restaurant. FSS-graded royalty reductions.

franchise_fee.value: $50,000 standard (20-year term). Prorated for shorter terms, minimum $15,000. Fully earned at signing, nonrefundable.

application_fee.value: $250 per individual applicant; $5,000 per entity applicant. Nonrefundable.

training_fee.value: $7,500 per new franchisee trainee (on transfer of first restaurant)

royalty.incentive_low: 2.5% Year 1 (2024 DIP); 2.0% Year 1 (DA A/B grade); additional 1%-5% for CYC if BKC remodels

advertising.value: Up to 4.5% of monthly Gross Sales (currently 4.5%)

investment_spending.value: Up to 2.0% of Gross Sales (DMA-level, collectively determined)

combined_ongoing_burden_estimate.value: Royalty 4.5% + Advertising 4.5% + IS up to 2.0% + Digital Services = approximately 11%+ of Gross Sales before rent, BK Foundation, gift card fees, and other charges
combined_ongoing_burden_estimate.note: This is a derived estimate, not a figure from the FDD.

approved_supplier_requirement.value: Most goods and services must meet BKC standards and be obtained from approved suppliers or distributors

affiliate_revenue_from_purchases.value: BKC and affiliates derive revenue from franchisee purchases. BKC negotiates arrangements and may receive rebates/incentives from suppliers.

double_drive_thru_requirement.value: Required for all facility types except food courts, in-lines, indoor MRS, Big Box Retail, and delivery facilities

initial_training.value: Mandatory training program. Training fee $7,500. Travel and living expenses $7,500-$25,000 depending on location and trips.

ongoing_training.value: BK University / eLearning Platform: $700/year/restaurant. Additional courses up to $25,000/person.

pos_system.value: BKC-approved hardware and software required. $20,000-$25,000 initial cost.

digital_menu_boards.value: Outdoor digital menu board required. Static menu board kit $200-$300/month if not installed.

digital_services.value: Digital App Services Agreement (Exhibit V). Mobile app, website, delivery, loyalty. $110/month + 1% digital sales, cap $4,500/year.

exclusive_territory.value: No exclusive territory. Target Areas are non-exclusive.

reserved_rights.value: BKC and affiliates may sell through grocery, convenience stores, internet, other channels. May develop competing restaurants nearby. Affiliates operate Tim Hortons, Popeyes, Firehouse Subs.

encroachment_protection.value: If BKC proposes to develop in your Target Area, you get option to develop that restaurant (if you meet requirements). Otherwise no protection.

term.value: 20 years standard. Shorter for non-traditional locations or shorter property control. Minimum 6 years.

renewal.value: No right of renewal. Option for successor Franchise Agreement (up to 20 years) if compliant, remodeled to current standards, sign then-current FA (may have materially different terms), pay successor fee.

transfer.value: BKC approval required. Transfer fee: $2,000 + $500/additional restaurant. Right of first refusal. General release required. Transferee must meet all requirements.

termination_with_cause.value: Immediate: bankruptcy, abandonment, health/safety, criminal conviction, fraud, misrepresentation. 30-day cure for most other defaults. Also for failure to meet remodel deadlines and repeated defaults.

post_term_noncompete.value: 2 years, within 5 miles of any BURGER KING restaurant (whether or not your former restaurant), for sale of hamburgers or similar quick-service food

mediation.value: Non-binding mediation required before suit on development disputes

during_term.value: No ownership, operation, or interest in any hamburger business except other franchised BURGER KING Restaurants.

post_term_fa.value: 1 year after sale/assignment/transfer/termination/expiration, at or within 2 miles of the Premises, no hamburger business.

post_term_da.value: 1 year, within 2-mile radius of any of your Burger King restaurants, following termination.

perpetual_section12.value: No use of BK System, marks, or trade secrets in any other restaurant operation, perpetually (during term and at all times thereafter).

cyc_enhancement.value: Managing Owner may not engage in or own any interest in ANY other business activity, be employed by any other business, or engage in activity impairing franchise obligations, until 5th anniversary of first BK FA (or until Promissory Note paid in full, whichever later, if BKC financing). Exception: <5% publicly traded securities.

value.CA: Post-term noncompete may not be enforceable under CA law (Bus. & Prof. Code anti-noncompete policy)

corporate_addendum_exception.value: Certain 'Contract Feeder' franchisees signing a Corporate Addendum may have the right under certain conditions to operate competing businesses.

count.value: 15 matters disclosed (14 pending + additional settled/resolved matters)

key_matters.case: First International Fund Ltd. v. BK Corporation (2013)
Arrington v. Burger King — no-poach class action (2018/2019)
key_matters.status: pending — class conditionally certified, appeal certified to 11th Circuit, cert petition to Supreme Court filed

key_matters.case: Plymouth County v. RBI — Carrols/CRG shareholder suit (2024)
key_matters.status: pending — motion to dismiss denied July 2025, trial set February 2027

key_matters.case: Pemberton v. RBI — website privacy/tracking class action (2025)
key_matters.amount: statutory, compensatory, and punitive damages sought
key_matters.status: pending — motion to compel arbitration denied, class certification ongoing

litigation_summary.note: Most disclosed litigation involves RBI affiliates rather than BKC directly. BKC states it is not a party to most matters.

bankruptcy_flag.value: No bankruptcy required to be disclosed

item18_public_figure_flag.value: No public figures disclosed. Item 18 states: 'We do not use any public figure to promote our franchise.'

item19_performance_summary.note: Item 19 has two sections: Section A (Sales Distributions by type) and Section B (Remodel Uplift by scope, DT conversion, image type, sales range, RTF enrollment, multi-year). Richest Item 19 seen.

speech_limitation_signal.value: Some current and former franchisees signed provisions restricting their ability to speak openly about their experience.

guarantor.value: RBI (general) or RBILP (for CA, IL, MD, ND, RI, VA, WA residents)

cash_flow_highlights.value: Not fully transcribed but available in Exhibit Q pages 812. Operating, investing, and financing activities for 3 years.

debt_structure.value: Total debt (current + long-term) = $13,677M. Leverage ratio ~2.8x debt/equity. Intangible assets + goodwill = $16.9B (69% of total assets). Heavily acquisition-driven balance sheet.

carrols_acquisition_impact.value: Carrols excluded from internal controls assessment. Carrols represented $1,988M total assets and $1,171M total revenues. Company restaurant sales surged from $271M to $1,592M. Operating lease assets +$730M YoY.

value: Carrols acquisition fair value (operating lease assets, lease liabilities, reacquired franchise rights)
Recoverability of Firehouse Subs brand ($816M indefinite-lived intangible)

unresolveds.issue: Item 8 — exact revenue/rebate amounts BKC derives from supply chain arrangements not quantified
Exhibit Q notes — debt covenants, segment reporting detail, related-party transactions not fully extracted
Development Agreement operative text (M1) — 24 pages not deeply read beyond Item 5/6/12 summaries
Exhibit G1 Lease/Sublease operative text — 41 pages not deeply read. Rent formula from Item 6 Fn4 surfaced.

contradictions.issue: Item 20 Table 1 shows total outlets at end 2023 = 6,778, but Table 3 franchised + Table 4 company-owned = 6,640 + 138 = 6,778. Consistent.

Item 20 text says 1,433 franchisees in Exhibit O3, which includes those who ceased operations for year ended 12/31/2024 OR did not communicate within 10 weeks. This is a larger number than the 49 non-renewals + 69 ceased ops in Table 3 (118 total). The 1,061 reacquired restaurants (Carrols) likely account for most of the difference.

key_overrides.value: CA: noncompete likely void, FL governing law may not apply, release cannot waive statutory claims. HI: mandatory FMV buyback on non-renewal. IL: IL law governs (overrides FL), litigation must be in IL. MD: release cannot waive MD-law claims, 60-day notice required. MN: MN law governs, no post-term damages on non-renewal. ND: extensive statutory protections (repurchase, noncompete limited, exclusive territory right). WA: WA law governs for WA franchises. NY: court-adjudicated good cause required for termination. RI: RI Franchise Investment Act compliance required. SD: 30-day cure for all defaults. VA: VA Retail Franchising Act protections.

franchise_level.value: Exhibit D3 — Personal guaranty by entity owner(s). Absolute, continuing, primary, unconditional guarantee of payment AND performance. Joint and several. Covers all debts/liabilities under all BKC Agreements. Irrevocable during term, survives death.

parent_level.value: Dual guarantor: RBI guarantees BKC obligations (general); RBILP guarantees for CA, IL, MD, ND, RI, VA, WA residents. Guarantee of Performance includes in Exhibit Q.

fee_confirmed.value: $110/month + 1% monthly Digital Sales, cap $4,500/year. Digital Sales excludes kiosk and credit card token transactions.

fee_adjustability.value: BKC can adjust fees unilaterally (increase or decrease) once per calendar year, no cap on magnitude, no franchisee consent required.

suspension_rights.value: BKC may suspend Cloud Services immediately upon written notice for security threats, legal proceedings, material breach, or non-payment. Suspension does not relieve franchisee of payment obligations.

data_rights.value: All data generated through Digital Platforms owned by BKC. Franchisee granted limited access during term only. BKC may use aggregated/anonymized data without restriction.

item21_structural_facts.reporting_entity: Restaurant Brands International Inc. and Subsidiaries
critical_audit_matters: Carrols acquisition fair value (operating leases, lease liabilities, reacquired franchise rights)
Recoverability of Firehouse Subs brand ($816M indefinite-lived intangible)
item21_structural_facts.carrols_exclusion: Carrols excluded from internal controls assessment: $1,988M total assets, $1,171M total revenues
item21_structural_facts.dual_guarantor: RBI (general) or RBILP (CA, IL, MD, ND, RI, VA, WA)

item1_structural_facts.controlling_interest: 3G Capital (through Burger King Holdings LLC → 3G Restaurant Brands Holdings LP)
item1_structural_facts.operating_affiliate: Carrols LLC (operates BK restaurants in US, being refranchised)

royalty_incentive_programs.crown_your_career_surcharge: 1% to 5% additional if BKC pays for remodel

balance_sheet_structure_2024.note: Heavily acquisition-driven balance sheet. Intangible-heavy. High leverage.

three_year_trend.note: 2023 net income benefited from large tax benefit (-$265M expense = benefit). 2024 tax expense normalized to $364M.

base_ongoing_fees.advertising: 4.5% of Gross Sales (max, currently at max)

penalty_and_default_fees.one_time_cure_mtra: Remaining franchise fee x uncommitted restaurants

item6_fee_detail.digital_services_adjustability: BKC can adjust once/year, no cap on magnitude, no franchisee consent

franchise_agreement_deep_read.source: Previous run D1/D2 deep read (verified against source)

d1_vs_d2_delta.value: D1 (Individual): Operating Partner must hold 50%+ equity. D2 (Entity): Managing Owner must hold 25%+ equity. D1 includes entity conversion provisions (Sec. 15.D). D2 allows parent-entity option via Corporate Addendum.

critical_findings.finding: BKC owns all data from POS, Additional Ordering Systems, and Additional Digital Systems. Net profits from selling data to third parties are BKC's sole property.
MOD Manual changes are deemed part of FA. BKC's 'good faith exercise of judgment' is the only constraint. No franchisee consent required.
65% media spending floor on advertising contribution.
ISP binding: if 66.7% of DMA signs ISP contracts, ALL franchisees in DMA bound to same terms. Capped at 2% of Gross Sales, 1-year max term.
BKC may publish aggregated franchisee financial data for any purpose without identifying specific franchisee.
Successor notice must be given during 4th year before expiration (not merely before expiration).
Post-transfer continuing liability extends for duration of installment payments. Installment notes must be subordinate to royalty/advertising and non-assignable.
$1,000 BK Foundation scholarship is mandatory annual purchase per restaurant, not optional.

lessor_contribution.note: BKC fee-owned properties only, FSS A/B grade required

rtf2_program_detail.fss_df_grade: $0 for both Franchisor and Lessor contributions
rtf2_program_detail.penalty_missed_deadline: +3.0% royalty surcharge on ALL Gross Sales, commencing on Remodel Deadline
rtf2_program_detail.cure_period: 1 year from Remodel Deadline with reduced contribution ($10K-$50K reduction by remodel type)
rtf2_program_detail.cross_default: Program default = default under all DAs, TRAs, MTRAs between Franchisee/affiliates and BKC. Survives expiration/termination.
rtf2_program_detail.general_release: Broad release of all claims from beginning of world through Effective Date. Includes CA Civil Code 1542 waiver.
rtf2_program_detail.completion_discretion: BKC has sole and absolute discretion over completion determination.

fuel_the_flame_detail.incremental_rate: 0.5% of Gross Sales above Legacy Rate

base_ebitda_condition.status: MET — FTF Rate Period has commenced

fuel_the_flame_detail.ebitda_rent_cap: Rental and leasing costs held to not exceed 7.4% above 2021 Base Year in EBITDA calculation

renewal_voting.who_votes: Participating US BK Restaurants + Company Restaurants, one vote per restaurant
renewal_voting.non_voters: Automatically counted with then-majority of votes actually cast

fuel_the_flame_detail.spending_restriction: FTF Funds (BKC Investment + Incremental Contributions) must be used solely for television media advertising expenses. Cannot be used for G&A, digital development, or agency fees.
fuel_the_flame_detail.default_cascade: 10-day cure. Uncured = FA default for each Participating Restaurant with no further cure opportunity.
fuel_the_flame_detail.transfer_successor_conditioned: Transfer and successor franchise conditioned on FTF participation — effectively mandatory for participating base.
fuel_the_flame_detail.renewal_period: Through 12/31/2028 if EBITDA condition met or survey approved

bkl_bkc_owned.value: Minimum annual rent = 10% of capitalized site/construction costs. Versus designated percentage of Gross Sales. 12% increase every 5 years.

bkl_bkc_leased.value: Minimum annual rent = 125% of BKC's rent + 10% of capitalized costs. Escalation: 125% of master lease escalation.

percentage_rent.value: 8.5% of monthly Gross Sales up to $133,333.33; 10% above $133,333.33 — in excess of monthly minimum rent

default_liability.value: Full remaining-term rent + re-letting costs + attorneys' fees

cyc_to_all_fa.value: Any BK FA default = all CYC-related agreement defaults

carrols_to_transaction_fa.value: Any Carrols transaction agreement default = FA default for all Purchased Restaurants in the transaction
carrols_to_transaction_fa.source_object: Exhibit X1 Section 7(b) / Previous run canonical

rtf2_to_all_da_tra_mtra.value: RTF2 MPA default = default under all DAs, TRAs, MTRAs between Franchisee/affiliates and BKC. Survives expiration/termination.
